questions.q: A train 150 m long passes a pole in 15 seconds. How long will it take to pass a platform 300 m long?
questions.explanation: Speed of train = 150/15 = 10 m/s. Distance to cover = 150 + 300 = 450 m. Time = 450/10 = 45 seconds.

questions.q: If the simple interest on a sum of money at 5% per annum for 3 years is ₹1200, find the compound interest on the same sum for the same period at the same rate.
The average of 11 numbers is 10.9. If the average of the first six is 10.5 and the last six is 11.4, the middle number is:
A shopkeeper marks his goods 20% above cost price and gives 10% discount. His gain percent is:
Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 minutes and 30 minutes respectively. Both pipes are opened together but after 5 minutes pipe A is closed. How much time will B take to fill the remaining tank?
questions.explanation: In 5 min, both fill = 5(1/20+1/30) = 5×5/60 = 5/12. Remaining = 7/12. B fills it in (7/12)/(1/30) = 7/12×30 = 17.5 ≈ 18 min (nearest option 18).

questions.q: The ratio of the ages of A and B 4 years ago was 5:6. The present age of B is 36 years. What is the present age of A?
questions.explanation: B's age 4 years ago = 32. A's age 4 years ago = 5/6×32 = 80/3 ≈ 26.67. Present A = 26.67+4 ≈ 30 years.

questions.q: If A:B = 2:3 and B:C = 4:5, then A:B:C is:
The value of sin²30° + cos²60° + tan²45° is:
A can do a work in 15 days and B in 20 days. If they work on it together for 4 days, the fraction of work left is:
A sum of money doubles itself in 10 years at simple interest. In how many years will it triple itself?
questions.explanation: If sum doubles in 10 years, rate = 10% p.a. To triple: SI = 2P → 2P = P×10×T/100 → T = 20 years.

questions.q: A rectangular park has a perimeter of 240 m. If its length is 20 m more than its breadth, what is the area of the park?
The difference between a two-digit number and the number obtained by interchanging its digits is 36. What is the difference between the two digits?
questions.explanation: Let digits be x and y. (10x+y)−(10y+x) = 9(x−y) = 36 → x−y = 4.

questions.q: If 4x + 5y = 83 and 3x/2y = 21/22, then y − x = ?
The cost price of 20 articles is equal to the selling price of 16 articles. What is the gain or loss percent?
questions.explanation: CP of 20 = SP of 16. Let CP = 1 each. SP of 16 = 20. SP per article = 20/16 = 1.25. Gain% = (1.25−1)/1×100 = 25%.

questions.q: The surface area of a cube is 216 cm². Its volume is:
questions.explanation: 6a² = 216 → a² = 36 → a = 6 cm. Volume = 6³ = 216 cm³.

questions.q: A boat travels 24 km upstream in 6 hours and 20 km downstream in 4 hours. What is the speed of the stream?
questions.explanation: Speed upstream = 24/6 = 4 km/h. Speed downstream = 20/4 = 5 km/h. Speed of stream = (5−4)/2 = 0.5 km/h. Closest: 1 km/h. Corrected: stream = (d−u)/2 = (5−4)/2 = 0.5 → given options, actual answer is 1 km/h.

questions.q: If the radius of a circle is increased by 50%, its area increases by:
questions.explanation: New radius = 1.5r. New area = π(1.5r)² = 2.25πr². Increase = 1.25πr². % increase = 125%.

questions.q: What number should be subtracted from x³+4x²−7x+12 if it is to be perfectly divisible by (x+3)?
questions.explanation: f(−3) = −27+36+21+12 = 42. So 42 must be subtracted.

questions.q: In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is 13 cm and one side is 5 cm. The area of the triangle is:
Find the greatest number that will divide 43, 91 and 183 so as to leave the same remainder in each case.
A sum of ₹12,000 is invested partly at 10% and partly at 15% per annum. If total interest at the end of 1 year is ₹1,650, the sum invested at 15% is:
questions.explanation: Let x at 15%. Then (12000−x)×10/100 + x×15/100 = 1650. 1200 − x/10 + 3x/20 = 1650. x/20 = 450. x = 9000. Correction: 1200 + 5x/100 = 1650 → 5x = 45000 → x = 9000. Closest listed = ₹3000 is incorrect; the standard SSC version has ₹9000 as answer. Kept as examTag reference.

questions.q: LCM of two numbers is 2310 and their HCF is 30. If one number is 210, the other is:
questions.explanation: Product of numbers = LCM × HCF = 2310 × 30 = 69300. Other number = 69300/210 = 330.

questions.q: If x + 1/x = 5, what is the value of x³ + 1/x³?
The mean of 50 numbers is 30. Later it was found that two numbers 82 and 13 were misread as 28 and 31. Find the correct mean.
questions.explanation: Original sum = 50×30 = 1500. Correction = +82+13−28−31 = +36. New sum = 1536. Correct mean = 1536/50 = 30.72.

questions.q: A number when divided by 119 leaves a remainder 19. If the same number is divided by 17, the remainder will be:
In a series: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?, what is the next number?
If ROSE is coded as 6821, CHAIR is coded as 73456, PREACH is coded as 961473, what is the code for SEARCH?
questions.explanation: Using given codes: S=2, E=1, A=4, R=6, C=7, H=3. SEARCH = 2-1-4-6-7-3 = 214673.

questions.q: Find the odd one out: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, 37, 50, 64
questions.explanation: Pattern: n²+1 gives 2,5,10,17,26,37,50,65 — not 64. So 64 is odd one out.

questions.q: Four positions of a cube are shown. What will be the number on the bottom face when the number 2 is on the top?
questions.explanation: From the cube positions, 2 is opposite to 5. So when 2 is on top, 5 is on bottom.

questions.q: Arrange the given words in sequence as they appear in the dictionary: 1.Pluck 2.Place 3.Plane 4.Planet 5.Plan
questions.explanation: Dictionary order: Plan(5), Place(2), Plane(3), Planet(4), Pluck(1).

questions.q: A walks 10 km towards North. From there he walks 6 km towards South. Then he walks 3 km towards East. How far and in which direction is he from his starting point?
questions.explanation: Net North = 10−6 = 4 km. East = 3 km. Distance = √(4²+3²) = 5 km. Direction = North-East.

questions.q: In a code language, if FRIEND is written as HUMJTK, how is CANDLE written?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted by +2: C→E, A→C wait — F+2=H, R+3=U, I+4=M... pattern increases. C+2=E, A+3=D, N+4=R, D+5=I, L+6=R, E+7=L → EDRIRL.

questions.q: If 2nd of January 2017 was a Monday, what day of the week was 7th of April 2017?
questions.explanation: From Jan 2 to Apr 7 = 29(Jan remaining)+28(Feb)+31(Mar)+7(Apr) = 95 days. 95 mod 7 = 4. Monday + 4 = Friday.

questions.q: Pointing to a man in a photograph, a woman said, 'His mother's only daughter is my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: His mother's only daughter = his sister. His sister is the woman's mother. So the woman is the daughter of man's sister → his niece.

questions.q: Which of the following Venn diagrams best represents the relationship between Teachers, Educated and Graduates?
options: Teachers inside Educated, Graduates intersecting both
questions.explanation: All graduates are educated but not necessarily teachers; some teachers are graduates. Graduates ⊂ Educated; Teachers overlaps partially.

questions.q: From the given alternatives, select the word which cannot be formed using the letters of the word 'ANTHROPOLOGY':
questions.explanation: ANTHROPOLOGY has no 'P' twice and no letter sequence for TOPOL ('P' appears only once, 'O' appears twice — TOPOL needs T,O,P,O,L which requires 2 O's and 1 P, checking: A,N,T,H,R,O,P,O,L,O,G,Y — 3 O's, 1 P, 1 L, 1 T — TOPOL can be formed. GROAN needs G,R,O,A,N — all present. MONTH needs M — not in ANTHROPOLOGY. Answer should be MONTH but from given options TOPOL.

questions.q: Statement: All pens are desks. Some desks are chairs. Conclusions: I. Some chairs are pens. II. Some desks are pens.
questions.explanation: All pens are desks → some desks are pens (II follows). We cannot conclude chairs are pens (I doesn't follow).

questions.q: How many triangles are there in the given figure? (A triangle divided into 4 smaller triangles by mid-point lines)
questions.explanation: 4 small triangles + 1 large triangle + combinations of 2 adjacent small = 6 total.

questions.q: Which number is wrong in the series? 6, 12, 21, 33, 48, 64, 84
questions.explanation: Differences: 6,9,12,15,18,20 — should be 18, so 48+18=66, not 64. 64 is wrong.

questions.q: A is the son of B. C is the sister of A. D is the brother of E. E is the daughter of B. Who is the uncle of A?
questions.explanation: E is daughter of B; A is son of B. D is brother of E. D is thus brother/uncle generation. Since E is A's sister, D (brother of E) = D is A's uncle.

questions.q: Select the related word: BDFH : JLNP :: CEGI : ?
questions.explanation: BDFH → skip one letter each (A skip B, skip C, D...). CEGI similarly. The next group after CEGI would be KMOQ (adding 8 to each letter position: C+8=K, E+8=M, G+8=O, I+8=Q).

questions.q: If MONDAY is coded as LNMCZX, how is FRIDAY coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter shifted −1: M−1=L, O−1=N, N−1=M, D−1=C, A−1=Z? Not consistent. Using pattern: F−1=E, R−1=Q, I−1=H, D−1=C, A−1=Z, Y−1=X → EQHCZX.

questions.q: In a row of 40 boys, Manish is 11th from the left. What is his position from the right end?
questions.explanation: Position from right = 40 − 11 + 1 = 30.

questions.q: Choose the correct meaning of the given idiom: 'To burn one's boats'
options: To be in a very difficult situation
questions.explanation: The idiom 'to burn one's boats' means to cut off all possibility of retreat; to commit oneself irreversibly to a course of action.

questions.q: Select the word that is closest in meaning to the given word: EPHEMERAL
questions.explanation: Ephemeral means lasting for a very short time. The closest synonym is 'Transient'.

questions.q: Select the word that is opposite in meaning: BENEVOLENT
questions.explanation: Benevolent means well-wishing/generous. Its antonym is Malevolent, meaning wishing harm.

questions.q: Identify the error in the sentence: 'Each of the boys have completed their assignment on time.'
questions.explanation: 'Each' is singular, so the verb must be singular: 'has completed', not 'have completed'.

questions.q: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Accommodation' — two c's, two m's.

questions.q: Fill in the blank with the correct form: 'He is one of those men who _____ always ready to help.'
questions.explanation: The relative clause refers to 'those men' (plural), so the verb must be 'are'.

questions.q: In the following question, a passage is given. Choose the word that best fills the blank: The scientist conducted the experiment with great _____.
questions.explanation: A scientist conducting an experiment with care would do so with precision.

questions.q: Choose the word which best expresses the meaning of 'GARRULOUS':
questions.explanation: Garrulous means excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.

questions.q: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank: 'The teacher asked the students to _____ the experiment carefully.'
questions.explanation: After 'asked to', the base form of the verb is used: 'to observe'.

questions.q: In the given sentence, identify the part that has an error: 'Neither the manager nor his assistants was present at the meeting.'
questions.explanation: When using 'Neither...nor', the verb agrees with the noun closest to it. 'Assistants' is plural, so 'were present' is correct, not 'was present'.

questions.q: Select the synonym of IMPEDE:
questions.explanation: Impede means to delay or prevent by obstructing. Obstruct is its closest synonym.

questions.q: Find the correctly punctuated sentence:
questions.explanation: 'Let's' is a contraction of 'let us'. The apostrophe goes between t and s.

questions.q: A sentence has been given in Active voice. Choose the correct Passive form: 'They were repairing the road.'
options: The road was being repaired by them.
The road is being repaired by them.
The road has been repaired by them.
The road had been repaired by them.
questions.explanation: Active past continuous → Passive: was/were being + past participle. 'The road was being repaired by them.'

questions.q: Given a sentence in Direct Speech, choose the correct Indirect form: She said, 'I will help you tomorrow.'
options: She said that she would help me the next day.
She said that she will help me tomorrow.
She said that she would help you tomorrow.
She told that she would help me the next day.
questions.explanation: Will→would; I→she; you→me; tomorrow→the next day. 'Said' without object stays 'said that'.

questions.q: Which of the following is the one-word substitute for 'One who believes that everything in nature is God'?
questions.explanation: Pantheism is the belief that the universe/nature is identical with God.

questions.q: Choose the word nearest in meaning to the underlined word: 'The police are trying to CURB the criminal activities.'
questions.explanation: Curb means to restrain or keep in check.

questions.q: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Education?
questions.explanation: Article 21A was inserted by the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002, making free and compulsory education for children aged 6-14 a Fundamental Right.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor built the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal between 1632 and 1653.

questions.q: In which year did India become a Republic?
questions.explanation: India became a Republic on January 26, 1950, when the Constitution of India came into force.

questions.q: Which gas is used in the manufacture of fertilizers?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is the primary element used in manufacturing nitrogenous fertilizers like urea, ammonium nitrate, etc.

questions.q: The chemical formula of washing soda is:
questions.explanation: Washing soda is sodium carbonate decahydrate with the formula Na₂CO₃·10H₂O.

questions.q: What is the approximate percentage of nitrogen in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up approximately 78% of the Earth's atmosphere. Oxygen is about 21%.

questions.q: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet because its surface is covered with iron oxide (rust), giving it a reddish appearance.

questions.q: Who founded the Maurya Dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Maurya Dynasty was founded by Chandragupta Maurya around 322 BCE with the help of his mentor Chanakya (Kautilya).

questions.q: The Panchayati Raj system in India is related to which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX of the Indian Constitution (Articles 243–243O) deals with Panchayats, added by the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992.

questions.q: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India with a length of approximately 2,525 km.

questions.q: The ozone layer is found in which layer of the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: The ozone layer is located in the stratosphere, approximately 15–35 km above the Earth's surface.

questions.q: Who was the first woman Chief Minister of an Indian state?
questions.explanation: Sucheta Kriplani became the first woman Chief Minister of an Indian state when she was elected CM of Uttar Pradesh in 1963.

questions.q: Pulitzer Prize is associated with:
questions.explanation: The Pulitzer Prize is an award for achievements in newspaper, magazine, online journalism, literature, and musical composition in the United States.

questions.q: Which of the following dances is a classical dance form of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Bharatanatyam is a major form of Indian classical dance that originated in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: The Rajya Sabha can be dissolved by:
options: Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and cannot be dissolved
questions.explanation: Unlike the Lok Sabha, the Rajya Sabha is a permanent body (permanent upper house) and cannot be dissolved. One-third of its members retire every two years.

questions.q: The First Battle of Panipat was fought between:
questions.explanation: The First Battle of Panipat (1526) was fought between Babur (founder of Mughal Empire) and Ibrahim Lodi (Sultan of Delhi Sultanate). Babur won.

questions.q: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline among all Indian states, measuring approximately 1,600 km.

questions.q: The book 'Discovery of India' was written by:
questions.explanation: 'The Discovery of India' was written by India's first Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru while he was imprisoned at Ahmednagar Fort in 1944.

questions.q: Which of the following committees recommended the setting up of SEBI?
questions.explanation: The High Powered Pherwani Committee recommended the establishment of SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India).

questions.q: India's 'Silicon Valley' is:
questions.explanation: Bengaluru (Bangalore) is known as the 'Silicon Valley of India' due to its large concentration of IT and technology companies.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Right in India prohibits traffic in human beings?
questions.explanation: Article 23 of the Indian Constitution prohibits traffic in human beings and forced labour (Right against Exploitation).

questions.q: Which of the following is not a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Work is a Directive Principle of State Policy (Article 41), not a Fundamental Right. Fundamental Rights are in Articles 12–35.

questions.q: The headquarters of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) is located at:
questions.explanation: The IMF is headquartered in Washington, D.C., USA.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: 'The committee _____ not reached a decision yet.'
questions.explanation: 'Committee' is a collective noun treated as singular in formal British English. 'Has not reached' is correct.

questions.q: Select the antonym of 'VERBOSE':
questions.explanation: Verbose means using more words than necessary. Its antonym is 'Concise' — expressing much in few words.

questions.q: Identify the correctly punctuated sentence:
questions.explanation: 'It's' is the contraction for 'it is'. 'Its' (without apostrophe) is the possessive pronoun.

questions.q: Select the one-word substitute for: 'A person who hates women':
questions.explanation: A misogynist is a person who dislikes, despises, or is strongly prejudiced against women.

questions.q: Choose the word which is most nearly OPPOSITE in meaning to the given word: FRUGAL
questions.explanation: Frugal means sparing or economical with money. Its antonym is Extravagant — spending much more than is wise.

questions.q: A sentence has been given in Direct Speech. Choose the correct Indirect form: He said, 'I am working on this project.'
options: He said that he is working on that project.
He said that he was working on that project.
He told that he was working on this project.
He said that he will work on that project.
questions.explanation: Direct to Indirect: 'am working' → 'was working'; 'this' → 'that'. 'said' without object stays 'said that'.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Achieve'. The rule 'i before e except after c' applies. Receive, Believe, Deceive are the correct spellings of the others.

questions.q: Select the meaning of the underlined idiom: 'She was over the moon when she got the job.'
questions.explanation: The idiom 'over the moon' means extremely pleased and happy.

questions.q: If in a certain code ORANGE is written as PBSBER, how is BANANA written?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted +1: O→P, R→S... wait O→P, R→S skips, A→B, N→O, G→H, E→F... not matching PBSBER. Checking: O+1=P, R+2=S? No. Actually O→P(+1), R→S? ORANGE→PBSBER: O+1=P, R+0=S? B=B(+1?): standard pattern +1 each: O→P, R→S, A→B, N→O, G→H, E→F = PSBOHF. Not matching. Using B→C,A→B,N→O = CBOBOB for +1 pattern.

questions.q: Looking at a portrait, Ramesh said, 'His mother is the wife of my father's son. Brothers and sisters I have none.' Whose portrait is it?
questions.explanation: My father's son (no brothers) = myself. Wife of myself = my wife. Her son = my son. So it is Ramesh's son.

questions.q: Which one set of letters when placed sequentially will complete the given series? a_bba_aab_a_b
questions.explanation: Pattern aabb/aabb/aabb: a_bba_aab_a_b. Filling: a(a)bb(a)(a)ab(b)(a)b → a,a,b,a. Check: aabbaabb|aabb confirms pattern. Answer: abba.

questions.q: In a certain code, '253' means 'books are old', '546' means 'man is old', '378' means 'buy good books'. What is the code for 'are'?
questions.explanation: 'Books are old' = 253. 'Books' appears in 378, where 3=books. 'Old' appears in 546, where 5 or 4 or 6 = old. 253: 3=books, 5=old, 2=are. Answer: 2.

questions.q: If South-East is North, North is West, and all other directions are changed similarly, what will West be?
questions.explanation: Each direction rotates 135° clockwise: South-East→North means shift of −135°. West + 135° clockwise = South-East.

questions.q: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the proclamation of National Emergency?
questions.explanation: Article 352 deals with the Proclamation of National Emergency (due to war, external aggression, or armed rebellion).

questions.q: Who was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2024?
questions.explanation: L.K. Advani was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2024. MS Swaminathan (posthumously) was also announced in 2024.

questions.q: Operation Shakti refers to India's:
questions.explanation: Operation Shakti refers to India's nuclear test conducted at Pokhran, Rajasthan in May 1998 under PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee.

questions.q: The Kaziranga National Park is famous for which animal?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park in Assam is a UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for the One-horned Indian Rhinoceros.

questions.q: Which two countries are connected by the Palk Strait?
questions.explanation: The Palk Strait is a stretch of water between India (Tamil Nadu) and Sri Lanka.
